blocks:
Ayahs

إِنَّا فَتَحْنَا لَكَ فَتْحًا مُّبِينًا (۱)
Indeed We have inaugurated for you a clear victory, (1)
لِّيَغْفِرَ لَكَ ٱللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِن ذَنۢبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ وَيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكَ وَيَهْدِيَكَ صِرَٰطًا مُّسْتَقِيمًا (۲)
that Allah may forgive you what is past of your sin and what is to come, and that He may perfect His blessing upon you and guide you on a straight path, (2)
وَيَنصُرَكَ ٱللَّهُ نَصْرًا عَزِيزًا (۳)
and Allah will help you with a mighty help. (3)

Key Points

There is disagreement among exegetes regarding what is meant by the fath mubin (manifest victory) in the first verse of Surah Al-Fath. A group, such as Alusi, Abul Futuh, Allamah Tabataba'i, and Fayd Kashani, relate it to the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. Another group, such as Shaykh Tusi, Zamakhshari, and Fakhr Razi, consider it related to the Conquest of Mecca, and both groups have cited narrations. The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah is important because it was the beginning of the Conquest of Mecca and the significant victories that followed. Furthermore, until that day, the polytheists only thought of destroying the Muslims and did not acknowledge their status. But by accepting the peace, they essentially accepted the Muslims, which was, in fact, a great victory in the socio-political arena. It was a manifest victory because, in the sixth year, 1,400 people were with the Prophet, but two years later, 10,000 people accompanied the Prophet in the Conquest of Mecca.

In the system of Divine guidance, there is no ambiguity; everything is clear and illuminating, including: His Messenger is illuminating. «رسول مبین»(1) His Quran is clear and illuminating. «قرآن مبین»(2) It is expressed in a clear and plain language. «هذا لسان عربى مبین»(3) His warnings are also without ambiguity. «نذیر مبین»(4) Even His war and peace are clear. «فتحا مبینا» Therefore, if any group opposes in the face of all this clarity, their deviation is also very manifest. «و مَن یعص اللّه و رسوله فقد ضلّ ضلالاً مبینا»(5)

In these verses, the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is addressed six times: «فتحنا لك... لیغفر لك، ذنبك، علیك، یهدیك، ینصرك» which indicates the special status of the Prophet of Islam before Allah.

The word dhamb (ذَنب) means the trail, effects, and consequences of anything, and it is mostly used regarding sin and its negative consequences. There is no doubt that any truth-seeking movement is unpleasant for the deviated, and they count that call as a sin. And against its callers, they initiate all kinds of problems, worries, harassment and torture, false accusations, conspiracies, disputes, and rumors. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: No one's sin was greater in the eyes of the polytheists than the Prophet's sin, because the Prophet fought against idol worship. While the polytheists had 360 idols before the Conquest of Mecca, with the Conquest of Mecca and the powerful victory of the Prophet and the Muslims, all [these perceived] errors were covered up. (6)

Allah, in the second year of the Hijra when the Qibla changed, in the sixth year when the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah occurred, and in the eighth year when Mecca was conquered, promises the completion of the favor in the future, saying: «یتمّ نعمته» (He will complete His favor). But during Hajjat al-Wida' (the Farewell Pilgrimage), He fulfilled those promises, and by announcing the appointment of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام to the Imamate, He completed the favor and said: «الیوم اكملت لكم دینكم و اتممت علیكم نعمتى» (This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you). (7)

The Incident of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah

The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله set out for Mecca in the month of Dhu al-Qa'dah in the sixth year of the Hijra with the intention of performing Hajj, and encouraged the Muslims to participate in this journey. Finally, with a group of 1,400 people, they moved toward Mecca wearing the garments of Ihram. News reached the polytheists of Mecca, and near Mecca (the village of Hudaybiyyah), they blocked the path of the Muslims and prevented their entry into Mecca. In this incident, representatives from both sides traveled back and forth for negotiations. When the representative of the polytheists saw that the Muslims were so devoted to the Prophet that they collected the drops of water from the Prophet's ablution for blessing (tabarruk), he told the polytheists: With this love and devotion that the people have for Muhammad, you cannot separate them from him. Uthman went to Mecca as the ambassador of the Muslims for negotiation, but a rumor spread that he had been killed in Mecca. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, to prepare the Muslims, renewed his allegiance with his companions under a tree; this allegiance was named Bay'at al-Ridwan (The Pledge of Good Pleasure). After a few days, Uthman returned safely, and a peace treaty consisting of several articles was prepared in the presence of representatives from both sides, written by Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, and signed by both parties. Among these [articles] was that the two groups committed: that no war would take place between them for ten years. The Muslims should return from that very spot and come to Mecca the following year for Umrah for three days. By the command of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, they sacrificed the camels they had brought for Hajj right there, shaved their heads, exited the state of Ihram, and returned to Medina. Although the Muslims did not perform Hajj, this peace treaty and its provisions—the cessation of war for ten years and the freedom to perform the Umrah rituals—was considered a manifest victory for the Muslims. Because it was an end to the manifest hostility of the polytheists, opened a path for the propagation of the religion, allowed the Muslims to renew their strength, and paved the way for the Conquest of Mecca.

1) Dukhan, 13. 2) Hijr, 1. 3) Nahl, 103. 4) A'raf, 184. 5) Ahzab, 36. 6) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 7) Ma'idah, 3.

Messages

  1. Victory is not always achieved through war and struggle. «انّا فتحنا...» (Allah called the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah or the Conquest of Mecca a victory.)
  2. We must consider our success and victory to be under the shadow of Allah's grace, not the result of our own thought, plan, strategy, or power. «انّا فتحنا...»
  3. The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is the object of Allah's special favor. «فتحنا لك... لیغفر لك... یهدیك... ینصرك» (The addressee is the Prophet himself.)
  4. Everything is in the hands of Allah. «فتحنا، لیغفر، یتمّ، یهدیك، ینصرك» (Unless Allah wills it, nothing comes to fruition.)
  5. Victory over the enemy is not the ultimate goal, but a prelude to receiving other favors. «فتحنا، لیغفر، و یتمّ نعمته علیك»
  6. If we work for Allah, He Himself will answer our enemies and silence them. «لیغفر لك اللّه ما تقدّم من ذنبك و ما تأخّر»
  7. Even the Prophet walks the straight path through Divine guidance and success. «یهدیك صراطاً مستقیماً»
  8. What Allah bestows is the most prominent, clearest, and best. The victory is manifest, the favor is complete, the guidance is to the straight path, and His help is mighty. «فتحا مبینا، یتم نعمته، یهدیك صراطا مستقیما، نصراً عزیزاً»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Fath

This Surah has twenty-nine verses and was revealed in Medina in the sixth year of the Hijra, after the incident of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. The main topics of this Surah concern the glad tidings of the Conquest of Mecca, issues related to the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, Bay'at al-Ridwan, the obstructionism of the hypocrites and those who failed to participate in Jihad, and finally, a portrait of the companions of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ ٱلسَّكِينَةَ فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ لِيَزْدَادُوٓا۟ إِيمَـٰنًا مَّعَ إِيمَـٰنِهِمْ ۗ وَلِلَّهِ جُنُودُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (٤)
It is He who sent down composure into the hearts of the faithful that they might enhance in their faith. To Allah belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (4)

Key Points

«سكینه» (Sakīnah) means stillness and peace. When Allah grants this state to anyone, it brings many results for him, including: he does not fear blame, he possesses reliance (Tawakkul), if he loses something he does not grieve, and if he gains something he does not transgress or disobey. The welcome or indifference of people, fame or obscurity, and poverty or wealth have no effect on him. Just as casting fear into the hearts of the disbelievers is one of the Divine aids, «سنلقى فى قلوب الّذین كفروا الرّعب»(8) tranquility is also a Divine blessing that He sends down upon the hearts of the believers. «أنزل السّكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین»

8) Aal-i-Imran, 151.

Messages

1- Spiritual tranquility is only found under the shade of Divine favors, and no other person or thing can give peace to man. «هوالذى أنزل السّكینة»

2- Receiving Divine favors requires merit. «أنزل السّكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین...»

3- The vessel for tranquility is the heart of the believer, and the means to tranquility is the remembrance of Allah. «أنزل السّكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین...» (As we read elsewhere: «الّذین آمنوا و تطمئنّ قلوبهم بذكر اللّه ألا بذكر اللّه تطمئنّ القلوب»(9))

4- Faith (Iman) has degrees and is capable of increasing or decreasing. «لیزدادوا ایمانا»

5- One stage of faith is the prerequisite for achieving tranquility, and another stage of faith is tranquility itself. «لیزدادوا ایماناً» (Initially, faith is necessary so that Allah may send down tranquility upon the heart «انزل السّكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین», and the result of tranquility is the elevation of capacity and the increase of faith.)

6- Paying attention to the Divine hosts in the heavens and the earth is a means of tranquility. «أنزل السّكینة... للّه جنود السّموات»

7- The entire existence constitutes the armies of Allah. «للّه جنود السّموات و الارض»

8- Divine favors and Allah’s assistance are based on knowledge and wisdom. «و كان اللّه علیماً حكیماً»

9) Ra'd, 28.

Ayah

لِّيُدْخِلَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا وَيُكَفِّرَ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ فَوْزًا عَظِيمًا (٥)
That He may admit the faithful, men and women, into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], and that He may absolve them of their misdeeds. That is a great success with Allah. (5)

Key Points

The word fawz (فوز), which appears in the Quran accompanied by the words kabīr (كبیر - great), mubīn (مبین - manifest), and ʿaẓīm (عظیم - tremendous), means attaining goodness and felicity. In the first and second Ayahs, Allah bestowed four blessings upon His Prophet, and in the fourth and fifth Ayahs, four blessings and favors that Allah granted to the believers are mentioned. The blessings upon His Prophet were: manifest victory, forgiveness, the completion of the favor, and the guidance of the Prophet and His support for him under the shadow of the manifest conquest (fath mubīn). As for the blessings He granted to the believers: sakīnah (tranquility) and peace, the increase of faith, entry into Paradise, and the covering of evil deeds. (10) Paradise is a place of purity and cleanliness, and the slightest contamination has no place there. First, the slips of the inhabitants of Paradise must be purified, and then they enter Paradise. «لیدخل... یكفّر عنهم سیئاتهم» Of course, it is possible that the intent of takfīr (covering/expiation) is not the forgiveness of sins, but rather the covering of sins from the minds of the inhabitants of Paradise themselves, so that they are not ashamed or distressed in Paradise by remembering their past sins.

10) Tafsir al-Maraghi.

Messages

1- The descent of Divine tranquility (sakīnah) causes the increase of faith and provides the ground for entry into Paradise. «انزل السّكینة... لیزدادوا ایماناً... لیدخل المؤمنین و المؤمنات جنّات»

2- Supportive and like-minded women attain fawz (felicity) and salvation alongside men. «لیدخل المؤمنین و المؤمنات جنّات» (Although the manifest conquest (fath mubīn) was achieved by men, their like-minded women who fulfill their duties and are content with the participation of their husbands and children in the front lines and Jihad, share in the rewards alongside the men.)

3- The meaning of faith is not that no slip or error ever occurs from a person. «المؤمنین و المؤمنات... یكفّر عنهم سیئاتهم»

4- Believers become worthy of entering Paradise through Allah's forgiveness and overlooking their slips (not merely through their own deeds). «لیدخل المؤمنین... یكفّر عنهم»

5- The great and true felicity and salvation of the believer is to have a tranquil heart in this world and Paradise in the Hereafter. «أنزل السّكینة... لیدخل... فوزاً عظیما» (It is a complete and valuable felicity that encompasses both this world and the Hereafter.)

Ayahs

وَيُعَذِّبَ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقَـٰتِ وَٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ وَٱلْمُشْرِكَـٰتِ ٱلظَّآنِّينَ بِٱللَّهِ ظَنَّ ٱلسَّوْءِ ۚ عَلَيْهِمْ دَآئِرَةُ ٱلسَّوْءِ ۖ وَغَضِبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَعَنَهُمْ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ جَهَنَّمَ ۖ وَسَآءَتْ مَصِيرًا (٦)
That He may punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the polytheists, men and women, who entertain a bad opinion of Allah. For them shall be an adverse turn of fortune: Allah is wrathful with them and He has cursed them, and prepared hell for them and it is an evil destination. (6)
وَلِلَّهِ جُنُودُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا (۷)
To Allah belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (7)

Key Points

"دائرة السوء" (Dā'iratu s-Sū') means a bitter and widespread incident/calamity. (11) Following the mention of the fourfold favors of Allah upon the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and also upon the believers in the preceding verses, this verse states several threats against the hypocrites and the polytheists. In this verse, the name of the hypocrite comes before the polytheist, suggesting that hypocrisy (Nifāq) is worse than polytheism (Shirk), and the hypocrite is worse than the polytheist. It is possible that the punishment (Azhāb) intended for the hypocrites and polytheists is not the punishment of the Hereafter, but rather the suffering and hardships of the path of hypocrisy and polytheism that would afflict them after the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and the Conquest of Mecca. We read in a Hadith: Allah does not punish any servant after repentance and seeking forgiveness, except due to having ill thoughts (sū'u ẓann) about Allah and not having hope in Him. Thereupon, the Imam recited this verse. (12) The phrase "للّه جنود السّموات و الارض" (To Allah belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth) appeared once for showing favor to the believers in verse 4, and it appears again in this verse for wrath and punishment. However, where Allah sends hosts to bring good to the believers, He describes Himself with the words "علیما حكیما" (All-Knowing, All-Wise), but where He sends hosts for wrath and punishment, He praises Himself with "عزیزاً حكیما" (All-Mighty, All-Wise). This is so that He may tell the believers: I know all your deeds, and tell the opponents: You are dealing with an invincible Power. But in both cases, Our action is wise (Ḥakīmānah), not based on revenge, malice, resentment, or the like.

11) Mufradāt Rāghib. 12) Biḥār, Vol. 67, p. 399.

Messages

1- Encouraging the righteous and threatening the deviants are necessary side by side. «لیدخل المؤمنین... و یعذّب المنافقین...»

2- An incident may be beneficial for some and a punishment for others. The Manifest Victory (Fatḥ Mubīn) was a divine favor for the believers and a punishment for others. «لیغفر... لیدخل... یعذب»

3- Women are like men in acquiring virtues or vices. «المؤمنین و المؤمنات... المنافقین و المنافقات و المشركین و المشركات»

4- Having ill thoughts (sū'u ẓann) about Allah is the characteristic of hypocrites and polytheists; otherwise, believers have hope, love, and reliance (Tawakkul) upon Allah. «الظانین باللّه ظنّ السوء»

5- Hypocrites and polytheists are united in thought and companionship in mischief and deviation. «الظانین باللّه ظنّ السّوء»

6- The believer, due to his faith, is in tranquility (Sakīnah) and peace, but the polytheist and the hypocrite, due to deviation and ill thoughts about Allah, are afflicted by anxiety and restlessness. «دائرة السوء»

7- The hypocrite and the polytheist are far removed from Divine Mercy in this world and the Hereafter. «غضب اللّه علیهم و لعنهم و اعدّ لهم جهنّم و ساءت مصیراً»

8- Allah carries out His work through means and causes. «للّه جنود...»

9- Allah has abundant hosts both for showing favor to the believers and for bringing down wrath upon those who incur His anger. «للّه جنود...»

10- The display of Divine Power is effective in generating piety (Taqwā) and love. «للّه جنود السموات و الارض»

Ayahs

إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ شَـٰهِدًا وَمُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا (۸)
Indeed We have sent you as a witness, and as a bearer of good news and warner, (8)
لِّتُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتُعَزِّرُوهُ وَتُوَقِّرُوهُ وَتُسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا (۹)
that you may have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and that you may support him and revere him, and that you may glorify Him morning and evening. (9)

Key Points

«تُعزّروه» (tu'azzirūh) derives from «تعزیر» (ta'zīr), meaning to restrain or prevent, and the intention is that you protect the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله from any harm or annoyance. It is possible that the intent of «شاهد» (shāhid, witness) is the presentation of a complete model. When it is said that the Prophet is a witness, it means He is the perfect and complete example of a human being. The phrases «تعزّروه» (tu'azzirūh) and «توقّروه» (tuwaqqirūh) might refer to Allah, or they might refer to the Prophet, though certainly, honoring the Prophet is honoring Allah.

Messages

1- The Prophet is a supervisor and witness over our deeds at all times. «شاهداً» (It is presented in the indefinite and absolute form.)

2- The duty of the Prophet is supervision over deeds, giving warnings and glad tidings. «شاهداً و مبشّراً و نذیراً» And the duty of the people is to defend the sanctity of Allah and the Prophet of God and to honor Him. «لتؤمنوا باللّه و رسوله و تعزّروه و توقّروه»

3- Among the principles of divine education and guidance is introducing a model alongside glad tidings and warnings. «شاهداً و مبشّراً و نذیراً»

4- Glad tidings and warnings, encouragement and punishment, are two essential human needs for finding and choosing the correct path. «مبشّراً و نذیراً»

5- The prerequisite of faith is support for and protection of the sanctity of the religion and the Messenger of God. «تعزّروه»

6- Aiding and protecting the Prophet must be accompanied by reverence and honor, stemming from love and knowledge. «توقّروه»

7- We must always remember Allah, and the beginning and end of the day are the best times for remembrance (dhikr) and supplication (du'a). «تسبّحوه بُكرة و اصیلا»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُبَايِعُونَكَ إِنَّمَا يُبَايِعُونَ ٱللَّهَ يَدُ ٱللَّهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ ۚ فَمَن نَّكَثَ فَإِنَّمَا يَنكُثُ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِمَا عَـٰهَدَ عَلَيْهُ ٱللَّهَ فَسَيُؤْتِيهِ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۱٠)
Indeed those who swear allegiance to you, swear allegiance only to Allah: the hand of Allah is above their hands. Then whosoever breaks his oath, breaks it only to his own detriment, and whoever fulfills the covenant he has made with Allah, He will give him a great reward. (10)

Key Points

"Bay'ah" (allegiance/oath) means covenant and commitment. The one who gives bay'ah to another is, as it were, putting his wealth and life up for sale and transaction for the sake of the other's goals. (13) As Jābir ibn Abdullāh al-Ansārī says: On the Day of Bay'at al-Ridwān (Pledge of Acceptance) under the tree, we pledged allegiance to the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to the point of self-sacrifice and not fleeing. To express favor toward the loyalists, two phrases are mentioned in this Ayah: one is "يداللّه فوق ايديھم" (The Hand of Allah is above their hands) and the other is "أجراً عظيماً" (a great reward). But to express wrath toward the covenant-breakers, one phrase is mentioned: "ينكث على نفسه" (he breaks it only to his own detriment).

13) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Belief in the Prophet, supporting Him, and honoring Him must be a permanent commitment and ongoing process, not a temporary or seasonal program. "تُعَزِّرُوهُ وَتُوَقِّرُوهُ... إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبَايِعُونَكَ" (The present tense form indicates permanence and continuity).

2- Taking the oath of allegiance (bay'ah) from the people does not contradict Monotheism (Tawhid) or reliance on Allah (Tawakkul). "يُبَايِعُونَكَ"

3- The action and decision of the Prophet are the very will and desire of Allah, and the Prophet does not act upon anything except the Divine Will. "الَّذِينَ يُبَايِعُونَكَ إِنَّمَا يُبَايِعُونَ اللَّهَ"

4- Allegiance to the Prophet is allegiance to Allah. "يُبَايِعُونَكَ... يُبَايِعُونَ اللَّهَ"

5- The leader of the Muslims must emphasize the loyalty of the people during critical situations and renew the covenant with them. (It is understood from the context and the occasion of revelation that the Prophet of Islam took the oath of allegiance from the people during the critical situation before the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah.) (14)

6- Supporting the Divine leadership entails the support of Allah. "يُبَايِعُونَكَ... يَدُ اللَّهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ"

7- Do not be deceived by the allegiance and support of the people, for the power of Allah is above every power. "يَدُ اللَّهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ"

8- Allah has no need for anyone's support; therefore, beware of breaking the covenant, for you are dealing with a God Whose power is above all powers. "يَدُ اللَّهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَمَنْ نَكَثَ..."

9- Breaking the covenant is self-detriment. "فَإِنَّمَا يَنْكُثُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ"

10- State the outcome for the good and the bad so that people may choose for themselves by comparison. "فَمَنْ نَكَثَ... وَمَنْ أَوْفَى..."

14) Tafsir Rāhnamā.

Ayah

سَيَقُولُ لَكَ ٱلْمُخَلَّفُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَعْرَابِ شَغَلَتْنَآ أَمْوَٰلُنَا وَأَهْلُونَا فَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لَنَا ۚ يَقُولُونَ بِأَلْسِنَتِهِم مَّا لَيْسَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ ۚ قُلْ فَمَن يَمْلِكُ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا إِنْ أَرَادَ بِكُمْ ضَرًّا أَوْ أَرَادَ بِكُمْ نَفْعًۢا ۚ بَلْ كَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًۢا (۱۱)
The Bedouins who had stayed back [from joining the Prophet in his ‘umrah journey to Makkah] will tell you, ‘Our possessions and families kept us occupied. So plead [to Allah] for our forgiveness!’ They will say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. Say, ‘Whether He desires to cause you harm or desires to bring you benefit, who can be of any avail to you against Allah[’s will]? Indeed Allah is well aware of what you do.’ (11)

Key Points

The term al-A'rāb (Bedouins) refers to desert dwellers who shun society and are far removed from culture. There is no doubt that these individuals are not merely the opposite of city dwellers; rather, anyone who disobeys the command of the Prophet is, in a sense, far removed from culture and civilization. Those who avoid the battlefield justify their flight with excuses: Sometimes they say: The weather is hot. «لاتنفروا فى الحرّ»(15) Sometimes they say: The enemy is numerous and our strength is little. «لاطاقة لنا»(16) Sometimes they say: Our houses lack doors, walls, or protection, and we are worried. «انّ بیوتنا عورة»(17) Sometimes they say: We fear that by looking at the Roman girls (during the Battle of Tabuk), we will fall into temptation and sin. So, do not cast us into temptation by commanding us to participate in the war. «لاتفتنّى...»(18) Sometimes they say: Our wealth, possessions, and family have preoccupied us and prevented our presence in the war. «شَغلتنا أموالنا و اهلونا» Usually, those who avoid Jihad justify fear in the guise of caution, greed and avarice in the guise of securing the future, weakness of spirit with modesty and shyness, laziness and incompetence in the guise of asceticism (zuhd), and their own weakness and inability by attributing it to Divine decree (Qada wa Qadar) and submission to the will of Allah.

15) Tawbah, 81. 16) Baqarah, 249. 17) Ahzab, 13. 18) Tawbah, 49.

Messages

1- One of the factors leading to weakness in Jihad is cultural deficiency and the low level of public culture. «المخلّفون من الاعراب»

2- The leader must be forward-looking, anticipate the words and movements of opponents and hypocrites, and have a ready answer. «سیقول... قل...»

3- Those who lag behind and disobey justify their actions. «شغلتنا أموالنا»

4- Attention to and attachment to economic and family matters prevent a group from fulfilling the Divine command and participating in Jihad. «شغلتنا أموالنا و اهلونا»

5- Lagging behind from Jihad is a sin. «فاستغفر لنا»

6- The prayer and intercession of the Prophet on behalf of others are answered and accepted. Therefore, people sought intercession (tawassul) through the Prophet. «فاستغفر لنا»

7- Sometimes exposure (of secrets) is necessary. «یقولون بالسنتهم ما لیس فى قلوبهم»

8- Fleeing from Jihad does not guarantee the safety of wealth and life from danger. «فمن یملك لكم من اللّه شیئاً ان اراد بكم ضرّا»

9- Defending the faith (maktab) is obligatory, even if we incur harm and loss. «أراد بكم ضرّا»

10- It seems that the hypocrites and those who avoid Jihad do not know that Allah knows everything about them; otherwise, they would not display so much hypocrisy. «بل كان اللّه بما تعملون خبیرا»

Ayahs

بَلْ ظَنَنتُمْ أَن لَّن يَنقَلِبَ ٱلرَّسُولُ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِيهِمْ أَبَدًا وَزُيِّنَ ذَٰلِكَ فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ وَظَنَنتُمْ ظَنَّ ٱلسَّوْءِ وَكُنتُمْ قَوْمًۢا بُورًا (۱۲)
Rather, you thought that the Apostle and the faithful will not ever return to their folks, and that was made to seem decorous to your hearts; you entertained evil thoughts, and you were a ruined lot. (12)
وَمَن لَّمْ يُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ فَإِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ سَعِيرًا (۱۳)
Those who have no faith in Allah and His Apostle [should know that] We have prepared a blaze for the faithless. (13)

Key Points

The word "būr" (بور) means bankruptcy, disgrace, and destruction. Human nature recognizes deeds as bad and ugly, but Satan and the lower self make them appear beautiful to man so that he commits them. Those who avoided Jihad imagined that the Muslims would be defeated and all killed; thus, they brought fear, stinginess, deprivation, and disgrace upon themselves.

Messages

  1. Allah is aware of the inner thoughts of people and will one day unveil them. «بل ظننتم»

  2. Many calculations and analyses that confuse or captivate man are not based on reality. «بل ظننتم»

  3. Sometimes, suspicion (sū' al-ẓann) and deviant thoughts influence a person so much that they lead him to certainty and ultimately to disobedience. «بل ظننتم ان لن ینقلب الرسول»

  4. Fear of defeat is one of the factors for fleeing Jihad. «بل ظننتم ان لن ینقلب الرسول...»

  5. Excessive attention to family is a factor for fleeing Jihad. «لن ینقلب... الى أهلیهم ابداً»

  6. Attention to family sometimes gains such prominence and value in a person's eyes that the individual is willing to abandon God and His Messenger for their sake. «زیّن ذلك فى قلوبكم»

  7. Suspicion (sū' al-ẓann), false assumptions, and incorrect calculations render the human heart barren and ruin his personality. «ظننتم ظنّ السوء و كنتم قوماً بورا»

  8. Disobedience to the commands of the Messenger of Allah indicates a lack of true faith or is a precursor to faithlessness. «من لم یؤمن باللّه و رسوله»

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ يَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۱٤)
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth: He forgives whomever He wishes, and punishes whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (14)

Key Points

In cases where the Quran states: "Allah forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills," considering that Allah is both Just and Wise, the meaning is that in instances of grace and forgiveness, man has made himself worthy of receiving Divine forgiveness, and in instances of wrath and punishment, man has rendered himself ineligible.

Messages

1- Forgiveness is the right of the One who possesses absolute power. «للّه ملك السّموات... یغفر»

2- Allah's grace prevails over His wrath. «یغفر لمن یشاء و یعذّب», ("Yaghfir" [He forgives] comes before "Yu'adhdhib" [He punishes]).

3- Fear and hope are necessary side by side. «یغفر... یعذّب»

4- The path of repentance is open to everyone. «كان اللّه غفوراً رحیما»

5- In addition to forgiving the past, Allah secures man's future through His mercy. «یغفر... غفورا رحیما»

6- Allah's forgiveness and pardon stem from grace, kindness, and mercy, not from need or expectation (of return). «غفورا رحیما»

Ayah

سَيَقُولُ ٱلْمُخَلَّفُونَ إِذَا ٱنطَلَقْتُمْ إِلَىٰ مَغَانِمَ لِتَأْخُذُوهَا ذَرُونَا نَتَّبِعْكُمْ ۖ يُرِيدُونَ أَن يُبَدِّلُوا۟ كَلَـٰمَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ قُل لَّن تَتَّبِعُونَا كَذَٰلِكُمْ قَالَ ٱللَّهُ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ فَسَيَقُولُونَ بَلْ تَحْسُدُونَنَا ۚ بَلْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۱٥)
[In the near future] when you will set out to capture booty, those who stayed behind [in this journey] will say, : ‘Let us follow you.’ They desire to change the word of Allah. Say, ‘You will never follow us! Allah has said thus beforehand.’ Then they will say, ‘You are envious of us.’ Indeed, they do not understand but a little. (15)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Shān Nuzūl), it is stated that Allah commanded the Prophet that only those who had been present at Hudaybiyyah were entitled to participate in the Khaybar campaign. However, the defaulters of Hudaybiyyah wanted to change this command. Despite not being present at Hudaybiyyah, they decided to participate—either to compensate for their past failure or because significant spoils were to be gained at Khaybar. The revelation of this Ayah exposes their true nature (19). Ayahs 12 and 13 cited the reason for their non-participation in Hudaybiyyah as suspicion towards Allah and weakness of faith. Those who were absent from Hudaybiyyah wanted to show, by participating in Khaybar, that they possessed faith, that there was no suspicion involved, and that Allah's statement concerning them was untrue.

19) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Compensating for failures and strengthening the morale of the believers is necessary. Allah compensates for the difficulties of Hudaybiyyah with the spoils of Khaybar. «انطلقتم الى مغانم» (You set forth to spoils) instead of «انطلقتم الى الجهاد» (You set forth to Jihad).

2- A group of opportunists declare their presence when the spoils are being distributed. «مغانم لتأخذوها ذرونا نتّبعكم» (Spoils for you to take, let us follow you).

3- Changing and altering the word and command of Allah is one of the desires of the defaulters. «یریدون ان یبدلوا كلام اللّه» (They wish to change the Word of Allah).

4- A special account must be made for the combatants, and opponents must be rejected. «قل لن تتّبعونا» (Say: You shall not follow us).

5- Base your own words upon the Word of Allah. «قل... كذلكم قال اللّه» (Say... Thus has Allah said).

6- One should not fear threats and accusations. «فسیقولون بل تحسدوننا» (Then they will say: Nay, but you envy us).

7- The enemy's method of behavior and slogans are dependent on circumstances and are constantly changing. Initially, they intend to change the Word of Allah, but as soon as they fail, they change their position and accuse the believers of envy. «فسیقولون... ذرونا نتّبعكم... فسیقولون بل تحسدوننا».

8- Those who are superficial in their outlook are led astray in analyzing and interpreting the consequences of their own improper behavior. Instead of recognizing their poor track record as the reason for losing divine favors, they attribute it to the envy of the believers. «بل تحسدوننا بل لایفقهون الا قلیلا» (Nay, but you envy us. Nay, but they understand not save a little).

Ayah

قُل لِّلْمُخَلَّفِينَ مِنَ ٱلْأَعْرَابِ سَتُدْعَوْنَ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ أُو۟لِى بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ تُقَـٰتِلُونَهُمْ أَوْ يُسْلِمُونَ ۖ فَإِن تُطِيعُوا۟ يُؤْتِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ أَجْرًا حَسَنًا ۖ وَإِن تَتَوَلَّوْا۟ كَمَا تَوَلَّيْتُم مِّن قَبْلُ يُعَذِّبْكُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۱٦)
Say to the Bedouins who stayed behind, ‘[Later on] you will be called against a people of a great might: they will either embrace Islam, or you will fight them. So if you obey, Allah will give you a good reward; but if you turn away like you turned away before, He will punish you with a painful punishment.’ (16)

Messages

1- Keep the door of discussion and dialogue open for opponents and those who lagged behind. «قل للمخلّفین»

2- One of the manifestations of the miraculous nature of the Quran at the time of its revelation is its giving news of the future. «ستدعون»

3- Let us keep the path of return and reparation open for those who lagged behind, and not boycott them forever. «ستدعون الى قوم»

4- Do not regard the enemy as simple or weak. «اولى بأس شدید»

5- Combatants must fight consciously and must have identified the enemy and its equipment beforehand. «أولى بأس شدید»

6- Let us overlook enmities when stating the facts (about the enemy's strength). «أولى بأس شدید»

7- The strength and power of the enemy is not an obstacle to performing one's duty and obligation. «أولى بأس... تقاتلونهم»

8- Muslims must be at a level of military power that compels the powerful enemy to embrace Islam. «تقاتلونهم أو یُسلِمون»

9- In the battlefield, the inner self and essence of individuals are revealed, and sincerities and hypocrisies become manifest. «تطیعوا... تتولوا»

10- Good deeds and virtues erase past evils. «قل للمخلّفین... ان تطیعوا یؤتكم اللّه اجراً حسناً»

11- In propagation and education, prioritize encouragement and reward over threat and reprimand. The phrase «اجراً حسناً» comes before «عذاباً الیماً».

12- Encouragement and punishment are necessary side by side. «أجراً حسناً... عذاباً الیماً»

Ayah

لَّيْسَ عَلَى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلْأَعْرَجِ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلْمَرِيضِ حَرَجٌ ۗ وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ يُعَذِّبْهُ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۱۷)
There is no blame on the blind, nor is there any blame on the lame, nor is there blame on the sick [if they are unable to go out with the troops to face the enemies]; and whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, He will admit him into gardens with streams running in them, and whoever refuses to comply, He will punish him with a painful punishment. (17)

Key Points

The criterion for exemption and the boundary of excuse for abandoning the battlefield (Jihad) is blindness, inability to walk (lameness), illness, and poor health, not short stature or similar issues. Although going to the battlefield is not obligatory for some groups, goodwill (sincerity) towards the fighters is necessary, just as Allah says in Surah At-Tawbah, Ayah 91: «لیس على الضعفاء و لا على المرضى... حرج اذا نصحوا للّه و رسوله»

Messages

1- The legislator must pay attention to all aspects of the law and establish special provisions for specific cases (such as the blind, the lame, and the sick). «لیس على الاعمى حرج...»

2- Disabled individuals should not feel inferior; there is no sin or fault upon them. (The word «حَرَج» [blame/difficulty], in literary terms, is an indefinite noun in the context of negation, which implies a general meaning.) «لیس على الاعمى... على الاعرج حرج»

3- Divine obligations are commensurate with one's power and ability. «لیس على الاعمى... حرج»

4- Those who are excused are not deprived of Paradise, provided they are obedient to the extent of their ability. «لیس على الاعمى... مَن یطع اللّه...»

5- The Prophet is infallible (Ma'sum), because obedience to him is coupled with obedience to Allah. «مَن یطع اللّه و رسوله»

6- The primary criterion for entering Paradise is obedience, although intercession (Shafa'ah) is sometimes effective. «من یطع... یدخله جنّات»

Ayahs

۞ لَّقَدْ رَضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ يُبَايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ ٱلشَّجَرَةِ فَعَلِمَ مَا فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ فَأَنزَلَ ٱلسَّكِينَةَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَأَثَـٰبَهُمْ فَتْحًا قَرِيبًا (۱۸)
Allah was certainly pleased with the faithful when they swore allegiance to you under the tree. He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down composure on them, and requited them with a victory near at hand (18)
وَمَغَانِمَ كَثِيرَةً يَأْخُذُونَهَا ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا (۱۹)
and abundant spoils that they will capture, and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (19)

Key Points

The custom was that after a contract or transaction was concluded, the two parties would shake hands, which was called Bay'ah (allegiance/pledge). However, subsequently, Bay'ah has been applied to any type of agreement, commitment, or acceptance of a covenant that is declared through a handshake. Bay'ah is a religious commitment; fulfilling it is obligatory, and breaking it is forbidden and incurs punishment. The people's pledge of allegiance (Bay'ah) to the Prophet and the Imam does not contradict their appointment and designation by Allah. This is because Allah designates the Prophet or the Imam, and the people pledge allegiance to him to declare their obedience. The meaning of "الشجرة" (the Tree) is a tree in the region of Hudaybiyyah, beneath which the people pledged allegiance to the Prophet. This pledge was named Bay'at al-Ridwan (The Pledge of Good Pleasure) because Allah declared His satisfaction with those who pledged allegiance ("رضى اللّه").

Messages

1- Faith is acceptable to Allah only when it is accompanied by loyalty to His Prophet. «لله رضى اللّه عن المؤمنین اذ یبایعونك»

2- Religion is not separate from politics. Allah's satisfaction is with the believers who pledge allegiance (Bay'ah) to their Prophet regarding social and political matters. «رضى اللّه... اذ یبایعونك»

3- To record important events, mentioning the landmark (or sign) is necessary. «یبایعونك تحت الشّجرة»

4- Divine favors are specific to those whose actions stem from sincerity and truthfulness. «فعلم ما فى قلوبهم... فانزل السّكینة علیهم»

5- Tranquility (Sakīnah) is a Divine gift that is sent down by Allah only upon the believers. «فانزل السّكینة»

6- Pure and sincere intention does not contradict material gains. «فعلم ما فى قلوبهم... و مغانم كثیرة»

7- Loyalty to the Divine Messenger is the key to receiving worldly and otherworldly favors. «رضى اللّه... أنزل السّكینة... و مغانم كثیرة»

8- Spiritual blessings are superior to material blessings. First, He said: «رضى اللّه - انزل السّكینة», and then He said: «و مغانم كثیرة»

9- A single sincere action from a person results in multiple Divine favors. «یبایعونك - فانزل السّكینة... و أثابهم... و مغانم»

10- Tranquility is the prelude to victory. «فانزل السّكینة... و أثابهم فتحاً»

11- We must consider victories to be from the grace of Allah. «اثابهم فتحاً» Just as we read in the first Ayah of this Surah: «انّا فتحنا لك»

12- If we strive for the pleasure of Allah and make ourselves worthy and deserving through loyalty to His Prophet, the promises of Allah are wise, and He is Capable of granting those promises. «كان اللّه عزیزا حكیما»

Ayah

وَعَدَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ مَغَانِمَ كَثِيرَةً تَأْخُذُونَهَا فَعَجَّلَ لَكُمْ هَـٰذِهِۦ وَكَفَّ أَيْدِىَ ٱلنَّاسِ عَنكُمْ وَلِتَكُونَ ءَايَةً لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَيَهْدِيَكُمْ صِرَٰطًا مُّسْتَقِيمًا (۲٠)
Allah has promised you abundant spoils, which you will capture. He has expedited this one for you, and withheld men’s hands from you, so that it may be a sign for the faithful, and that He may guide you to a straight path. (20)

Key Points

In the two previous verses, He promised those who pledged allegiance to the Prophet a "near victory" (fath qarīb), which, according to some commentators, such as Tabari, Shaykh Tusi, Zamakhshari, Maraghi, and Tabarsi, refers to the conquest of Khaybar. The fortress of Khaybar was about two hundred kilometers from Medina, with high walls, and about ten thousand Jews lived there, cultivating the surrounding lands. This very strong fortress had a heavy and unusual gate that required forty men to move it. Since Khaybar had become a base for enemies and a spy nest for opponents of Islam, the Muslims went there one month after the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. Initially, the command of the Muslims was held by individuals who could not achieve success, until the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Tomorrow, I shall give the banner to a man whom Allah and His Messenger are pleased with and who loves them. The next day, He gave the banner to Imam Ali عليه السلام. Finding the way to the Straight Path (Sirāt al-Mustaqīm) is the greatest divine blessing and favor, and Allah has always commanded man towards it, and all Muslims and believers must request guidance to that path from Allah day and night during prayer and supplication. Even though Allah says to the Prophet of Islam: «انّك لمن المرسلین، على صراط مستقیم» (20) (You are indeed one of the Messengers, upon a Straight Path); yet, the Prophet is commanded to ask Allah for guidance to the Straight Path in His prayers and say: «اهدنا الصّراط المستقیم» (Guide us to the Straight Path). Considering that supererogatory prayers (Nawāfil) were also obligatory upon the Prophet, and out of the 51 units (rak'āt) of obligatory and recommended prayers, Sūrat al-Ḥamd must be recited and this request made in 44 units—and this request comes from the Messenger of Allah, whose path and actions are guaranteed and secured by Allah—this demonstrates the danger of deviation and slipping in all human conditions.

(20) Yā Sīn, verses 3-4.

Messages

1- In war and Jihad, acquiring spoils (booty) from the enemy is permissible and is one of Allah's promises. «وعدكم اللّه مغانم كثیرة»

2- The expansion and development of the Muslims' economy is a divine blessing and gift, and it creates motivation. «وعدكم اللّه مغانم كثیرة»

3- What the Muslims acquired as spoils in places like Khaybar is not the entirety of Allah's promises. «فعجّل لكم هذه»

4- Haste is praiseworthy in some instances. «فعجّل لكم هذه»

5- Spoils are pleasant when accompanied by security. «كفّ ایدى النّاس عنكم»

6- The enemy withdrawing their hands from you is a divine blessing. «كفّ ایدى النّاس عنكم»

7- Do not consider events and encounters bitter; rather, each one, in its place, is a sign of Allah's demonstration of power. «لتكون آیة للمؤمنین»

8- Spoils, security, and guidance are the rewards of the believers. «مغانم... كف ایدى... یهدیكم»

9- Wealth and riches are a dangerous pitfall, and divine grace occurs when wealth is accompanied by guidance. «مغانم كثیرة... یهدیكم»

10- Requesting guidance to the Straight Path is always necessary, even after pledging allegiance to the Messenger of Allah, achieving victory, acquiring spoils, and attaining Allah's pleasure. «و یهدیكم صراطا مستقیما»

Ayah

وَأُخْرَىٰ لَمْ تَقْدِرُوا۟ عَلَيْهَا قَدْ أَحَاطَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرًا (۲۱)
And other [spoils as well] which you have not yet captured: Allah has comprehended them, and Allah has power over all things. (21)

Key Points

The Quran has stated repeatedly: ease accompanies hardship. «انّ مع العُسر یُسرا»(21) «سیجعل اللّه بعد عسر یسرا»(22) «سنیسره للعسرى»(23) The early years of the Prophetic mission (Bi'that), which were accompanied by various slanders, tortures, and conspiracies, and the early years of the Hijra (migration), which brought many wars, deaths, and injuries, were difficult and exhausting years. But the final years of the Prophet's life brought comfort, victory, and honor, as this Surah has indicated so far: «فتحاً قریبا», «ینصرك اللّه نصراً عزیزا», «اثابهم فتحاً قریبا», «مغانم كثیرة» and «و اخرى لم تقدروا علیها».

21) Sharh, 5 and 6. 22) Talaq, 7. 23) Layl, 10.

Messages

1- The promise of attaining future spoils (Ghanā'im) is based on Divine knowledge and power. «و اُخرى لم تقدروا علیها قد احاط اللّه...»

2- Do not attribute the acquisition of spoils to your own cleverness. «و اُخرى لم تقدروا علیها»

3- Alongside successes, we should not be heedless of our weaknesses. (In previous verses, we read: «فتحا قریبا... مغانم كثیرة», and in this verse, He says: «لم تقدروا علیها»)

4- We must have faith in Allah's promises. «وعدكم اللّه مغانم كثیرة... قد احاط اللّه بها»

5- The verses of the Quran are a foundation for reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) and the training of monotheistic individuals (Muwahhidūn). «وعدكم... فعجّل... كفّ... یهدیكم... احاط اللّه... و كان اللّه على كل شى ء قدیرا»

6- One should rely on the promise of someone who possesses strong backing. «و كان اللّه على كل شى ء قدیرا»

Ayah

وَلَوْ قَـٰتَلَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوَلَّوُا۟ ٱلْأَدْبَـٰرَ ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا (۲۲)
If the faithless fight you, they will turn their backs [to flee]. Then they will not find any protector or helper. (22)

Key Points

If we consider these verses to be related to the continuation of the event of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, the meaning of the Ayah becomes that the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was not based on your inability or military weakness, but rather for the sake of the system's interest (maslahat al-nizam). Even at Hudaybiyyah, if you had engaged in war, you would have been victorious and your enemies would have fled. On the Day of Judgment, it will become clear that the disbelievers have nothing, and no one will accept their guardianship (wilayah) or help (nusrah). But in contrast, the believers have everything, even if they had nothing in this world. Imam Husayn عليه السلام says to Allah in the Supplication of 'Arafah: «ماذا فَقَد مَن وَجدك و ماذا وَجد مَن فقدك» (What has he lost who found You, and what has he found who lost You?) Whoever has Him, what does he lack? And whoever lacks Him, what does he have?

Messages

1- Weak Muslims attained such honor that their enemies could not bear to engage them in conflict. «ولو قاتلكم... لولّوا»

2- Whoever does not have Allah, has no helper. «لا یجدون ولیاً ولا نصیرا»

Ayah

سُنَّةَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِى قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ ٱللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا (۲۳)
[It is] Allah’s precedent that has passed before, and you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent. (23)

Key Points

This Ayah is similar to Ayah 21 of Surah Al-Mujadilah, where He says: «كتب اللّه لاغلبنّ أنا و رُسلى» (Allah has decreed: "I and My Messengers will surely prevail.") Allah has thus decreed that We and Our Prophets will certainly be victorious. History and historical events are governed by laws, and factors cause their growth or stagnation, honor or humiliation, and poverty or wealth. And the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is the victory of truth over falsehood.

Messages

1- Historical events are not sparks or accidents; rather, they are an ongoing, pre-planned process and law. «سنّة اللّه الّتى قد خلت»

2- Divine traditions (Sunan) and laws are beyond time, place, and limited human intellect, and they are not based on trial and error; therefore, they are comprehensive and unchanging. «لن تجد لسنّة اللّه تبدیلاً»

3- Divine laws do not become obsolete or ineffective over time. «لن تجد لسنّة اللّه تبدیلاً»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى كَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ عَنْهُم بِبَطْنِ مَكَّةَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَنْ أَظْفَرَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرًا (۲٤)
It is He who withheld their hands from you, and your hands from them, in the valley of Makkah, after He had given you victory over them, and Allah sees best what you do. (24)

Key Points

Apparently, the intention behind both sides withdrawing from war is the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah (Ṣulḥ al-Ḥudaybiyyah). This is because the polytheists of Mecca had prepared themselves for war, and the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله had also taken an oath of allegiance (Bay'ah) from the Muslims, and both sides were staunch enemies of one another. In the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, Allah, on the one hand, cut off the hand of aggression of the polytheists, and on the other hand, restrained the believers from killing the polytheists. This was a blessing at that time for two reasons: One was the preservation of the sanctity of the Sanctuary (Ḥaram) and Mecca, which should not be violated or damaged by the Muslims. The other was the ten-year ceasefire that was included in the peace treaty, and during this period, a great number of people joined the Muslims. Because the Muslims had not killed any of them, they performed a collective 'Umrah (minor pilgrimage) the following year, and the year after that, they conquered Mecca with the utmost splendor and awe. All of this was due to the blessings of that peace. If war and conflict had occurred at Hudaybiyyah and some polytheists had been killed, the path to peace, the ceasefire, the path to 'Umrah in subsequent years, the path to preserving the sanctity of Mecca, and perhaps the path to the future conquest of Mecca would have been closed. For this reason, the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah has been considered a manifest victory (Fatḥ Mubīn), a gateway for the dignified influence of Islam, and a catalyst for the rapid growth of Islam.

Messages

1- Allah's tradition (Sunnah) regarding His grace towards the believers is definite and certain. «و لن تجد لسنّة اللّه تبدیلاً و هو الذى كفّ أیدیهم عنكم»

2- All victories and advancements are due to Divine planning and will. «و هو الذى...»

3- Security and remaining safe from the enemy, especially while surrounded by the enemy, is a great blessing. «ببطن مكّة»

4- Sometimes peace is a sign of victory. «من بعد أن اظفركم علیهم»

Ayah

هُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوكُمْ عَنِ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ وَٱلْهَدْىَ مَعْكُوفًا أَن يَبْلُغَ مَحِلَّهُۥ ۚ وَلَوْلَا رِجَالٌ مُّؤْمِنُونَ وَنِسَآءٌ مُّؤْمِنَـٰتٌ لَّمْ تَعْلَمُوهُمْ أَن تَطَـُٔوهُمْ فَتُصِيبَكُم مِّنْهُم مَّعَرَّةٌۢ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۖ لِّيُدْخِلَ ٱللَّهُ فِى رَحْمَتِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ لَوْ تَزَيَّلُوا۟ لَعَذَّبْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۲٥)
They are the ones who disbelieved and barred you from the Sacred Mosque, and kept the offering from reaching its destination. And were it not for [certain] faithful men and faithful women, whom you did not know—lest you should trample them, and thus the blame for [killing] them should fall on you unawares; [He held you back] so that Allah may admit into His mercy whomever He wishes. Had they been separate, We would have surely punished the faithless among them with a painful punishment. (25)

Key Points

«Ma‘kūf» means forbidden and confined. «Al-Hadyu Ma‘kūfan» means that the animal designated for sacrifice is confined until it reaches the place of sacrifice. Mu‘takif (one who performs I'tikaf) is also said of someone who, for a sacred purpose, confines and commits himself in some way to a place or a thing. «Ma‘arrah» means harm and loss, and «Tazayyalū» means separation and segregation. The reason for accepting the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was that if Allah had commanded an attack to destroy the disbelievers who prevented you and your sacrificial animals from reaching the place of sacrifice, you would have invaded Mecca, and the defenseless Muslims residing there, whom you did not know, would have had their blood unjustly spilled during your assault and attack. In that case, you would have been liable for blood money. Thus, one of the reasons for the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was the preservation of the blood of the Muslims residing in Mecca. Imam Hassan عليه السلام said regarding the reason for accepting peace with Mu'awiyah: «Fa innī taraktuhu li-ṣalāḥi al-ummah wa ḥiqni dimā’ihā» (24) I abandoned the war for the welfare of the Ummah and the preservation of their blood. The case of the oppressed Muslims whom you might unknowingly kill is separate from the case of the Muslims who knowingly placed themselves on the front lines of disbelief and acted as shields for the disbelievers. In the first part of the Ayah, He states that an attack should not be launched, but in the second part, He permits the killing of Muslims who have made themselves shields for disbelief.

24) Kāmil Ibn Athīr, Vol. 3, p. 205; Bihār, Vol. 44, p. 30.

Messages

1- Despite all their disbelief and obstinacy, the polytheists of Mecca submitted to peace with the Muslims. «Kaffa aydiyahum... humu alladhīna kafarū»

2- Sacrifice holds a special place in the rituals of Hajj. (One of the crimes of the disbelievers mentioned is preventing the sacrificial animal from reaching the place of sacrifice.) «Wa al-hadya ma‘kūfan an yablugha maḥillah»

3- Explain the philosophy behind your war or peace to your followers. «Law lā rijālun mu’minūn» (Divine commands have reasons and philosophies, and the reason for the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was to prevent unknown Muslims from being killed.)

4- Observing the principle of prioritizing the more important over the important (al-ahammu wa al-muhimm) is a rational, religious, and customary rule. «Law lā rijālun...» (Yes, under certain circumstances, preserving the blood of Muslims is more important than attacking the disbelievers.)

5- The blood of a Muslim is sacred, regardless of whether they are male or female. «Rijālun mu’minūn wa nisā’un mu’mināt»

6- Harming Muslims, even unknowingly, entails responsibility and accountability. «Law lā rijālun... lam ta‘lamūhum»

7- Allah desires the domain of the believers to remain far from any stain of disgrace or ill repute. «Fa-tuṣībakum minhum ma‘arrah»

8- The leadership of the Islamic society must calculate all aspects, effects, and consequences of its commands. «Lam ta‘lamūhum an taṭa’ūhum fa-tuṣībakum minhum ma‘arrah» so that no harm is unintentionally inflicted upon the innocent.

9- Do not give the enemy an excuse. «Tuṣībakum minhum ma‘arrah bi-ghayri ‘ilm» If the command to attack Mecca had been issued and unknown Muslims were killed, the enemies would have used this action for their propaganda, saying that Muslims do not even show mercy to their own people.

10- Sometimes peace paves the way for the expansion of Islam. «Wa law lā rijālun... li-yudkhila Allāhu fī raḥmatihi man yashā’»

11- Disbelievers deserve certain Divine punishment. «Law tazayyalū la-‘adhdhabnā alladhīna kafarū minhum»

Ayah

إِذْ جَعَلَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلْحَمِيَّةَ حَمِيَّةَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِيَّةِ فَأَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُۥ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ وَعَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَأَلْزَمَهُمْ كَلِمَةَ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ وَكَانُوٓا۟ أَحَقَّ بِهَا وَأَهْلَهَا ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًا (۲٦)
When the faithless nourished bigotry in their hearts, the bigotry of pagan ignorance, Allah sent down His composure upon His Apostle and the faithful, and made them abide by the word of Godwariness, for they were the worthiest of it and deserved it, and Allah has knowledge of all things. (26)

Key Points

Ḥamiyyah (حمیّت) means warmth and heat, and it is also applied to intense anger and fanaticism (ta'aṣṣub). If fanaticism is based on ignorance (jāhiliyyah), it is reprehensible, but if it is based on religious zeal and insistence upon truth and logic, it is commendable.

In the incident of the Treaty of Ḥudaybiyyah, upon the command of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, Imam Ali عليه السلام began the peace treaty with "بسم اللّه الرحمن الرحیم" (In the Name of Allah, the Most Compassionate, the Most Merciful). The polytheists objected, saying they did not accept it, so the Imam changed it to "بسمك الّلهم" (In Your Name, O Allah). Then the polytheists objected to the phrase "رسول اللّه" (Messenger of Allah), demanding its removal. Once again, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله calmly accepted this change and removed the title "رسول اللّه." This was an example of the ḥamiyyah (fanaticism) of the polytheists and the descent of sakīnah (tranquility) upon the Prophet and his companions.

Islam combats the culture of Jāhiliyyah (the Age of Ignorance) in whatever form it takes: "ظنّ الجاهلیّة" (the suspicion of Jāhiliyyah) (25), "تبرّج الجاهلیّة" (the flaunting of Jāhiliyyah) (26), and "حمیّة الجاهلیّة" (the fanaticism of Jāhiliyyah).

The human heart is both the center for connection with Allah and the growth of virtues, and the ground for the spread of corruption:

Spiritual growth: "تطمئنّ قلوبهم بذكر اللّه" (their hearts find rest in the remembrance of Allah) (27), "أنزل السّكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین" (He sent down tranquility into the hearts of the believers) (28), and "فالّف بین قلوبكم" (and He united your hearts) (29).

Negative spread: "سنلقى فى قلوب الّذین كفروا الرُعب" (We shall cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve) (30), "یطبع اللّه على قلوب الكافرین" (Allah seals the hearts of the disbelievers) (31), and "اشمأزّت قلوب الّذین لا یؤمنون" (the hearts of those who do not believe shrink with aversion) (32).

In a ḥadīth, we read that the meaning of "كلمة التقوى" (the word of piety) is faith (33).

25) آل عمران، ۱۵۴. 26) احزاب، ۳۳. 27) رعد، ۲۸. 28) فتح، ۴. 29) آل عمران، ۱۰۳. 30) آل عمران، ۱۵۱. 31) اعراف، ۱۰۱. 32) زمر، ۴۵. 33) تفسیر نورالثقلین.

Messages

  1. The fanaticism of Jāhiliyyah is the cause of blocking the path to the mosque for believers. "صدّوكم عن المسجد الحرام... فى قلوبهم الحمیّة"
  2. What protects a person against the fanaticism of Jāhiliyyah is sakīnah (tranquility) and calmness. "حمیّة الجاهلیّة... فانزل اللّه سكینته"
  3. Moral corruptions grow and flourish in the environment of disbelief, and faith in Allah prevents the growth of negative instincts. "الّذین كفروا فى قلوبهم الحمیة..."
  4. Fanaticism and steadfastness are valuable if accompanied by reasoning and logic; what is criticized is the anger and fanaticism arising from Jāhiliyyah. "حمیة الجاهلیة"
  5. One should not retaliate in kind against ignorant behavior. "حمیةالجاهلیة... فانزل اللّه سكینته"
  6. Allah does not abandon the believers in the face of malice and fanaticism. "الحمیة الجاهلیة... فانزل اللّه سكینته"
  7. Calmness and sakīnah cannot be acquired through wealth, status, or number of people; rather, it is a Divine gift. "فانزل اللّه سكینته"
  8. Since Allah and the Prophet deemed peace at Ḥudaybiyyah better than attack and Jihād, the believers must adhere and be committed to it. "الزمهم كلمة التّقوى"
  9. Piety is valuable when it is permanent, not seasonal. "الزمهم كلمة التّقوى"
  10. To receive Divine favors, worthiness and merit must be established by the individuals themselves. "كانوا أحق بها و أهلها"
  11. Choices and priorities are not based on slogans and expectations, but must be conscious and knowledgeable. "كان اللّه بكل شى ء علیما"

Ayah

لَّقَدْ صَدَقَ ٱللَّهُ رَسُولَهُ ٱلرُّءْيَا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ لَتَدْخُلُنَّ ٱلْمَسْجِدَ ٱلْحَرَامَ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ءَامِنِينَ مُحَلِّقِينَ رُءُوسَكُمْ وَمُقَصِّرِينَ لَا تَخَافُونَ ۖ فَعَلِمَ مَا لَمْ تَعْلَمُوا۟ فَجَعَلَ مِن دُونِ ذَٰلِكَ فَتْحًا قَرِيبًا (۲۷)
Certainly Allah has fulfilled His Apostle’s vision in all truth: You will surely enter the Sacred Mosque, God willing, in safety and without any fear, with your heads shaven or hair cropped. So He knew what you did not know, and He assigned [you] besides that a victory near at hand. (27)

Key Points

In the sixth year of the Hijra, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله saw in a dream that the Muslims had entered the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) peacefully, having performed the rites of Umrah while shaving their heads and trimming their hair and nails. Upon hearing this dream, the Muslims became very happy and hopeful. After some time, the Prophet, accompanied by the Muslims, set out with the intention of performing the Hajj rites and reached Hudaybiyyah, just outside the gates of Mecca. The disbelievers became aware of the situation, blocked their path, and decided to slaughter the Muslims. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله took an oath of allegiance and loyalty from the Muslims accompanying Him, but finally, by the command of Allah, He made peace with the enemies and signed a peace treaty. This was because, during this attack, some unknown Muslims residing in Mecca would have been unjustly killed, and the Muslims would have unintentionally become entangled in paying blood money (diyah) and facing the taunts of the disbelievers, and many would have been consumed by the fire of discord (fitnah). There were blessings in this peace, and during this period, a large group joined Islam. However, some Muslims were worried about why the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله dream had not been fulfilled. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: It is not necessary for this dream to be interpreted and realized this year. In any case, the Prophet and His companions returned to Medina, and according to the terms of the peace treaty, Mecca was vacated for three days the following year, and the Muslims performed the rites of Umrah with great splendor and peace of mind. Of course, after some time, the disbelievers broke the covenant, and the Muslims conquered Mecca without bloodshed in the eighth year of the Hijra. Verses 11, 15, and 16 gave news of the future with the phrases «سیقول المخلّفون» (The stay-at-homes will say) and «ستدعون» (You will be called), just as this dream also gives news of the future. «فتح قریب» (a near victory) could refer to the conquest of Khaybar or Mecca, or it could be the Peace of Hudaybiyyah, which was, in reality, considered a victory for the Muslims. We read in a Hadith that Imam Ali صلى الله علیه وآله said: Allah mentioned shaving the head before trimming the hair; therefore, shaving is better than trimming. (34)

34) بحار، ج ۹۶، ص ۳۰۲.

Messages

1- The dreams of the Prophets are among the dreams that are certainly realized. «لقد صدق اللّه رسوله الرؤیا بالحق»

2- The certain entry of Muslims into the Sacred Mosque in a state of tranquility was a prophecy that came true and is one of the manifestations of the miraculous nature of the Quran. «لتدخلنّ المسجد الحرام»

3- Although the dream belongs to the Prophet, saying «ان شاء اللّه» (if Allah wills) is necessary when giving news of any matter that will occur in the future.

4- Shaving the head and trimming the hair and nails are among the rites of Hajj and Umrah. «محلقین رؤسكم و مقصّرین...»

5- External security is not enough; internal peace is also necessary. «آمنین - لا تخافون»

6- Man is not aware of all the effects and blessings, or the dangers and consequences, of his decisions, and he must be obedient to the command of Allah. The Muslims were thinking of entering Mecca, but Allah knew that the effects and blessings of the peace were greater and better. «فعلم ما لم تعلموا»

7- How often is a peace treaty the prelude to victory. «فجعل من دون ذلك فتحاً قریباً»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُۥ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ وَدِينِ ٱلْحَقِّ لِيُظْهِرَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلدِّينِ كُلِّهِۦ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًا (۲۸)
It is He who has sent His Apostle with guidance and the true religion, that He may make it prevail over all religions, and Allah suffices as witness. (28)

Key Points

The promise of the victory of Islam over all religions has been repeated many times in the Quran. This victory can be both in terms of scientific and logical superiority and dominance in argumentation, which has always been the case, and also a reference to the future of history when Islam will encompass the world and the inheritors of the earth will be the righteous servants, just as the power of Islam encompassed a large part of the earth in the first half-century of the Hijra. The future of history, in our view, is very bright and certain, and with the advent (Zuhur) of Hazrat Mahdi عليه السلام, the last infallible Imam from the Household of the Prophet, the world will be filled with justice and equity.

Messages

1- The dominance of truth over falsehood is an aspiration that only Allah can realize. «هو الذى»

2- The secret to the victory of Islam is Divine guidance and the truthfulness of the path. «ارسل رسوله بالهدى و دین الحق»

3- In difficult circumstances, giving glad tidings and hope is necessary. (When the disbelievers of Mecca do not allow Muslims to enter the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram), Allah gives the glad tidings of victory over the entire world, stating that you will prevail not only over the polytheists of Mecca but over all religions of the world.) «لیظهره على الدّین كلّه»

4- All past religions were meant for a specific period of time. «لیظهره على الدّین كلّه»

5- The Quran informs about the future of history and the globalization of Islam. «لیظهره على الدّین كلّه»

6- The acceptance of the people is not important; Allah being a Witness is sufficient. «كفى باللّه شهیداً»

7- If you believe in His Divinity, then trust in His promises as well. «كفى باللّه شهیداً»

Ayah

مُّحَمَّدٌ رَّسُولُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥٓ أَشِدَّآءُ عَلَى ٱلْكُفَّارِ رُحَمَآءُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ تَرَىٰهُمْ رُكَّعًا سُجَّدًا يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلًا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِضْوَٰنًا ۖ سِيمَاهُمْ فِى وُجُوهِهِم مِّنْ أَثَرِ ٱلسُّجُودِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مَثَلُهُمْ فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ ۚ وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِى ٱلْإِنجِيلِ كَزَرْعٍ أَخْرَجَ شَطْـَٔهُۥ فَـَٔازَرَهُۥ فَٱسْتَغْلَظَ فَٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَىٰ سُوقِهِۦ يُعْجِبُ ٱلزُّرَّاعَ لِيَغِيظَ بِهِمُ ٱلْكُفَّارَ ۗ وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِنْهُم مَّغْفِرَةً وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًۢا (۲۹)
Muhammad, the Apostle of Allah, and those who are with him are hard against the faithless and merciful amongst themselves. You see them bowing and prostrating [in worship], seeking Allah’s grace, and [His] pleasure. Their mark is [visible] on their faces, from the effect of prostration. Such is their description in the Torah and their description in the Evangel. Like a tillage that sends out its shoots and builds them up, and they grow stout and settle on their stalks, impressing the sowers, so that He may enrage the faithless by them. Allah has promised those of them who have faith and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward. (29)

Key Points

Rukka' (رُكّع) is the plural of Raki' (راكع), meaning one who performs Ruku' (bowing), and Sujjad (سُجّد) is the plural of Sajid (ساجد), meaning one who performs Sujud (prostration). These two words are used for those who are greatly devoted to Sujud, Ruku', and prayer, and who persevere in them. Suq (سوق) means the leg or the stem of a plant; Shat'a (شطأ) means the shoot or sprout of a plant; Azar (آزر) means to strengthen and assist; and Istaghlaz (استغلظ), derived from ghilzah (غلظت), means to become firm and strong. At the beginning of the Surah, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was addressed, "انّا فتحنا لك" (Indeed, We have granted you a clear victory). At the end of the Surah, the focus is also on the Messenger of Allah: "محمّد رسول اللّه". In this Ayah, the method of a believer's relationships is outlined: Relationship with strangers (non-believers): Ashidda' (اشداء) — severity and harshness. With their own people (believers): Ruhama' (رحماء) — affection and kindness. With Allah: Rukka'an Sujjadan (ركعا سجداً) — servitude and worship. With oneself: Yabtaghūn (یبتغون) — effort, striving, and hope for Allah's grace. The comparison of the Islamic society to an agricultural farm is noteworthy because: A. The crop comes from within the earth; belief also comes from within the human being. B. The crop grows in a suitable environment; the Islamic society also needs suitable conditions. C. The crop has natural, gradual, authentic, and stable growth; the growth of the Islamic Ummah also has stages, stability, and authenticity. When writing the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, as soon as Hadrat Ali عليه السلام wrote: Muhammad, Messenger of Allah, the representative of the disbelievers insisted that this phrase must be removed. Imam Ali عليه السلام was distressed by the removal of the title "Messenger of Allah," but Allah compensated for it by stating in the text of the Quran: "محمّد رسول اللّه". The companions of the Prophet are seeking this world and the Hereafter. Fadhl (فضل) refers to material blessings and provisions, and Ridwan (رضوان) refers to spiritual blessings and matters. Perhaps the intention is also that the companions of the Prophet do not value their own deeds, but rather rely on His grace, not on their actions. This Ayah refers both to the outward signs of the believer, which is humility and reverence in speech and conduct, "سيماهم فى وجوههم من اثر السجود" (Their mark is on their faces from the trace of prostration), and to the firmness and deep-rootedness of belief in the heart and soul of the believer, which is constantly growing and expanding. "كزرع اخرج شطأه..." (Like a seed which sends forth its shoot...). We read in the Gospel of Matthew: He put another parable before them, saying, "The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man took and sowed in his field. Though it is the smallest of all seeds, yet when it grows, it is the largest of garden plants (vegetables) and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and make nests in its branches." (35) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, explaining the Ayah "سيماهم فى وجوههم من اثر السجود," said: The intention is the effects of staying awake at night (in worship). "هو السّهر فى الصلاة" (It is staying up late in prayer.) (36) Islam is a comprehensive religion; in the political sphere, it is "اشداء على الكفّار" (severe against the disbelievers); in social ethics, it is "رحماء بينهُم" (merciful among themselves); and in the spiritual dimension, it is "تراهم ركعاً سجداً" (you see them bowing and prostrating).

35) Gospel of Matthew, Chapter 13, verses 31 and 32. 36) Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 229.

Messages

  1. Allah's promises are clear. (The Prophet whose message will prevail over all religions is Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah.) "ارسل رسوله بالهدى... محمد رسول اللّه..."
  2. The globalization of the message of Islam requires divine leadership and sincere companions. "لیظهره على الدین كله... محمد رسول اللّه و الّذین معه"
  3. One of the tools and methods of education is the introduction of role models. "محمّد رسول اللّه"
  4. Belief in the Messenger of Allah, "آمنوا به" (believe in Him), is the first step; the most important step is accompanying the Messenger. "والّذین معه" (and those who are with Him)
  5. Muslims must possess harshness, decisiveness, firmness, and severity against the enemy; and possess compassion, affection, mercy, kindness, love, and sentiment towards other believers. "اشداء على الكفّار رحماء بینهم"
  6. Indifference is forbidden; a Muslim must be the manifestation of love and hatred (for the sake of Allah). "اشداء على الكفّار رحماء بینهم"
  7. The criterion for love and wrath is faith and disbelief, not tribe, clan, wealth, or possessions. "اشداء على الكفّار رحماء بینهم"
  8. Worship is a permanent practice for the companions of the Prophet, not a seasonal act. "تراهم ركعا سجدا"
  9. In the Islamic society, prayer must be apparent and manifest, not hidden or concealed. "تراهم" (You see them)
  10. Ruku' (bowing) and Sujud (prostration) are central in prayer. "ركّعا سجّدا"
  11. Worship that is praised is that which is accompanied by frequency and perseverance. "ركّعا سجّدا"
  12. Parts of the current Torah and Gospel remain untouched and untampered with; therefore, the Quran refers to them in certain instances. "ذلك مثلهم فى التّوراة و... الانجیل"
  13. There were also prophecies in the previous heavenly books. The characteristics of the companions of the Prophet of Islam are mentioned in the Torah and the Gospel. "ذلك مثلهم فى التّوراة و... الانجیل"
  14. The examples in the heavenly books are natural, universal, understandable to all, accessible, and experiential. "كزرع اخرج..."
  15. Sustainable growth is that which is natural, independent, and gradual. "كزرع اخرج..."
  16. The Islamic society must be self-sufficient, strong, and independent so that it can stand on its own feet. "استوى على سوقه"
  17. Religion is not separate from politics. Alongside Ruku' and Sujud, decisive and enemy-crushing stances are necessary. "ركّعا سجّدا... لیغیظ بهم الكفّار"
  18. The quantitative and qualitative growth of Muslims angers the disbelievers. "اخرج شطئه فازره... لیغیظ بهم الكفّار"
  19. Ruku', Sujud, and accompanying the Messenger of Allah are not enough; all those matters must stem from faith and righteous deeds in order to receive a great reward. "الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات منهم مغفرة و اجراً عظیما"
  20. The reward of true believers is beyond imagination. "اجراً عظیما"
  21. Warding off harm takes precedence over gaining benefit. (First "مغفرة" [forgiveness], then "اجر عظیم" [great reward]).
